page: Microsoft Azure D128ds_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Ddsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 128 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 64 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The system is equipped with 512.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio. It includes a massive 7559 GB local storage disk and supports Premium Storage, though it does not contain any GPU accelerators.

Benchmark results position this instance in the top tier for multi-core processing and memory bandwidth. The multi-core CPU performance scored 145450.5 in stress-ng, and the Passmark CPU score reached 132598.72, both placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with read-only small block cached speeds at 10740.63 GB/sec and uncached large block write speeds at 255.02 GB/sec. Single-core performance is more moderate, with a stress-ng single-core score of 2265.39, placing it in the middle 50%.

This server is highly optimized for database operations, multi-threaded data decompression, and high-throughput web serving, as evidenced by its top-tier Redis SET performance of 204.83 million operations per second and static web serving throughput of 160.54 GB/sec. While it lacks dedicated GPU hardware, its advanced vector and matrix extensions allow it to achieve strong prompt processing speeds for large language models, though text generation speeds remain average. Given its high resource density, massive local storage, and top-tier multi-core capabilities, it provides a cost-efficient profile for enterprise-grade databases, heavy ETL pipelines, and highly parallelized compute workloads without the overhead of specialized GPU hardware.
description: Microsoft Azure D128ds_v7 is a high-capacity general-purpose virtual machine from the Ddsv7 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor. It features 128 dedicated vCPUs (64 physical cores) running at 4.2 GHz, 512.0 GB of RAM, and a substantial 7559 GB local storage disk. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance and exceptional memory bandwidth, as demonstrated by its high Passmark and stress-ng benchmarks. While single-core performance and LLM text generation speeds are average, its massive parallel processing capabilities and high-speed local storage make it highly efficient for demanding database operations, multi-threaded decompression, and high-throughput web serving. It offers a resource-dense, cost-efficient solution for enterprise workloads that require extensive CPU and storage capacity without dedicated GPU acceleration.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D128ds_v7 is a Ddsv7-family virtual machine with 128 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 7559 GB local storage, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for databases and parallel workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D128ds_v7 features 128 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 7559 GB local storage, optimized for high-throughput database and compute workloads.
tagline: A high-core, storage-dense virtual machine delivering exceptional multi-core processing and memory bandwidth for demanding enterprise databases and parallel workloads.
bullet_points: 128 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor at 4.2 GHz.
512 GB of system memory paired with a massive 7559 GB local storage disk.
Top-tier multi-core CPU performance and high-bandwidth memory architecture.
Excellent fit for high-throughput database operations and Redis deployments.
Supports advanced vector and matrix extensions for CPU-based inference.
